name: Example Design System
description: Example design system registry for demonstration and testing purposes. This registry is disabled by default and should not be used in production.

components.name: Primary Button
components.purpose: Primary action button for the most important user actions such as submitting forms, confirming critical actions, or completing a purchase.
whenToUse: Submitting forms or completing a transaction
Primary call-to-action on a page or modal
Triggering the main workflow or feature
whenNotToUse: Secondary or tertiary actions (use secondary/tertiary buttons instead)
examples: Submit button on a contact form
Confirm button on a delete confirmation modal
Checkout button on an e-commerce page
Save button on a settings page

accessibility.contrastRequirements: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text
accessibility.ariaGuidance: Use aria-label if button text is not descriptive enough. Ensure focus indicators are visible.

components.implementationNotes: Button text should be clear and action-oriented. Avoid generic labels like 'Click here'. Use semantic HTML <button> element, not <div> or <a> styled as button.
commonPitfalls: Using for secondary actions (creates visual hierarchy confusion)
Too many primary buttons on one page (dilutes importance)
Insufficient touch target size on mobile

components.name: Card
components.purpose: Container component for grouping related content such as articles, product information, user profiles, or dashboard widgets.
whenToUse: Displaying a collection of related information
Grouping content that belongs together semantically
Creating dashboard widgets or information panels
Organizing content in a grid or list layout
whenNotToUse: Simple text containers (use text component or frame)
examples: Product card in an e-commerce listing
Article preview card in a blog
User profile card in a directory

accessibility.contrastRequirements: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text content
accessibility.ariaGuidance: Use semantic HTML structure. If card is interactive, use role='button' or role='link' with appropriate keyboard handlers.

components.implementationNotes: Cards should have clear visual hierarchy with a title, optional subtitle, and body content. Use consistent padding and spacing. Ensure cards are keyboard accessible if interactive.
commonPitfalls: Too much content in a single card (breaks visual hierarchy)
Missing hover/focus states for interactive cards
Inconsistent spacing or padding across card variants

components.name: Text Input
components.purpose: Single-line text input field for collecting user text input such as names, emails, search queries, or short-form responses.
whenToUse: Collecting user text input (names, emails, addresses)
Form fields requiring short text responses
examples: Email address field in a signup form
Search bar in a navigation header
Name field in a profile form
Filter input in a data table

accessibility.contrastRequirements: WCAG AA: 4.5:1 contrast ratio for text and borders
accessibility.ariaGuidance: Always associate label with input using 'for' attribute or aria-labelledby. Use aria-invalid for error states. Provide aria-describedby for helper text or error messages.

components.implementationNotes: Use semantic HTML <input> element with appropriate type attribute. Always include a visible label. Provide placeholder text for guidance, but don't rely on it for required information. Validate input on both client and server side.
commonPitfalls: Not providing autocomplete hints where appropriate
